behaviors.description: Static prompt-template lint rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification:
Evidence: The behavior is a deterministic lint rule over prompt template text (no runtime harness, model, OS, or network boundary).
Lower-level miss-proof: No lower level exists than Unit for executable tests.
Rejected higher levels: The text says "static prompt-template lint check," which disqualifies System/Agentic/Workflow because no real infra or model output is required.
behaviors.test_description: Add unit tests for the lint function with fixtures containing leaked phase instructions copied from SKILL.md and near-miss safe templates; assert hard failure on leakage and pass on non-leakage variants.

behaviors.description: Fully assembled prompt from the real harness (after interpolation and fixtures) contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification:
Evidence: Failure appears at module handoff (template + interpolation + fixture expansion + final assembly), not in one isolated function.
Lower-level miss-proof: A Unit test of only the linter/template logic can miss leakage introduced during interpolation/assembly.
Integration-brake: No real HTTP/subprocess/OS primitive is required; no dependence on model judgment; single-stage assembly only.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt assembly pipeline in-process with production interpolation/fixture modules and assert the final rendered prompt has no SKILL.md phase-instruction leakage patterns.

behaviors.description: Test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context, not hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification:
Evidence: This behavior depends on wiring between invocation code, skill-file loader, and validation path.
Lower-level miss-proof: Unit tests of loader or validator alone cannot prove invocation uses file-backed skill context instead of hard-coded prompt rules.
Rejected higher levels: The behavior text focuses on source-of-truth wiring ("loads skill context from plugin/skill files") and does not require real model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute in-process invocation with temp plugin/skill files and assert the test path consumes file content as context (and fails if hard-coded fallback is used), while prompt text remains unchanged.

behaviors.description: Harness runs the same user-style prompt against intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variants without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification:
Evidence: The failure boundary is harness orchestration across prompt source, skill variant selection, and run configuration.
Lower-level miss-proof: Unit tests cannot catch regressions where prompt text is accidentally mutated when switching skill variants.
Rejected higher levels: The behavior is about controllable variant injection/orchestration, not correctness of model judgment itself.
behaviors.test_description: Create an integration test that executes two runs (baseline skill vs degraded/missing SKILL.md) with identical user-style prompt literal; assert only skill-context input changes and run setup succeeds in both cases.

behaviors.description: When relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification:
Evidence: Pass/fail depends on how the real model responds to changed skill context under identical prompt.
Lower-level miss-proof: Integration tests with stubbed outputs would always encode expected failure and cannot detect true model-behavior regression sensitivity.
Rejected Workflow: The behavior describes one behavioral outcome under one prompt condition, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run a live-model A/B behavioral smoke test with identical prompt text: baseline SKILL.md vs degraded/removed section; require the degraded variant to violate the structural behavioral expectation and fail.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before JSON parsing or extraction logic.
behaviors.justification:
Evidence: The failure is ordering/instrumentation across model-response handling, artifact writer, and parser modules.
Lower-level miss-proof: Unit tests of parser or writer alone cannot prove pre-parse capture ordering in the real harness path.
Integration-brake: No requirement for real network/subprocess or model-quality assertions; this is pipeline sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Drive the harness response-processing path with controlled model text and assert an artifact containing untouched raw output is written before parser/extractor is invoked (including malformed JSON cases).

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (schema paths, role-specific field placement), not only keyword checks.
behaviors.justification:
Evidence: This is assertion-engine logic and can be validated deterministically on crafted payloads.
Lower-level miss-proof: No lower level than Unit.
Rejected higher levels: Behavior text targets assertion semantics ("structural behavior constraints"), which can fail independently of harness I/O or live model calls.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the assertion helpers with synthetic outputs: cases that contain expected keywords in wrong structure/role must fail; structurally correct outputs must pass even with wording variation.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that keyword-only checks miss.
behaviors.justification:
Evidence: The behavior explicitly requires "live model execution" and depends on real model output characteristics.
Lower-level miss-proof: Deterministic fixtures can be made to satisfy either check and cannot validate that real outputs drift in structurally wrong but keyword-present ways.
Rejected Workflow: The behavior concerns one live execution/assertion stage, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live-model smoke runs on prompts known to be vulnerable to superficial keyword matches; verify structural assertions fail on regressed outputs where keyword-only checks would pass, and pass on correctly structured outputs.
